channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: CAPCOM Spotlight Returns on March 5 with PRAGMATA, Monster Hunter Stories 3 and More
item.content:encoded:
Today, Japanese developer and publisher CAPCOM announced that a new episode of its CAPCOM Spotlight series of livestreams will air on Thursday, March 5, at 2 PM Pacific Time.

This CAPCOM Spotlight will last approximately half an hour, focusing on the following upcoming and existing games:

  • Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection
  • PRAGMATA
  • Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection
  • Mega Man Dual Override
  • Street Fighter 6

Below, we've added a brief overview of each featured game, in release date order.

Street Fighter 6 is the only game that's already out. Launched in June 2023 to critical acclaim, it marked the studio's return to form for its flagship fighting franchise after the rocky reception to Street Fighter V. It introduced a new control scheme called Modern Controls to lower the barrier to entry for newcomers, a fully fleshed-out single-player World Tour mode, and a Battle Hub online space for players to gather and compete. The game has been regularly updated with new fighters and content, further cementing its status as one of the most successful entries in the series' history. In all likelihood, the CAPCOM Spotlight will feature a new DLC fighter announcement.

Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, the third entry in the spin-off RPG series set in the Monster Hunter universe, follows the two rival kingdoms of Azuria and Vermeil as they struggle to survive an environmental disaster known as the Crystal Encroachment. The players control the heir to Azuria, who teams up with Vermeil's princess, Eleanor, to combat the threat while navigating the fraught politics between the two nations. Like its predecessors, the game centres on becoming a Rider who bonds with and battles alongside monsters, using a turn-based combat system. It will be released on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Nintendo Switch 2, and PC via Steam on March 13, 2026.

Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection is a compilation of all three entries in the Mega Man Star Force series, originally released for the Nintendo DS between 2006 and 2008, now emulated and enhanced for modern platforms. All seven versions of the three games are included, alongside new difficulty and assist options, an autosave function, adjustable encounter rates, and fully rearranged soundtracks alongside the originals. Online card trading and battles are also supported, and the collection is scheduled for release on March 27, 2026.

PRAGMATA, on the other hand, is CAPCOM's long-awaited new sci-fi IP. It's an action-adventure game set on a lunar research station, following spacefarer Hugh and android Diana as they work together to fight the hostile AI controlling the station and attempt to return to Earth. X The game blends third-person gunplay with a unique real-time hacking mechanic: Diana uses her hacking capabilities to disable enemy armour while Hugh takes them down with firearms. Originally announced back in 2020 and delayed multiple times, it finally received a concrete release date at The Game Awards 2025: April 24, 2026. This means it's less than two months away from its debut.

Mega Man: Dual Override is the twelfth mainline entry in the classic Mega Man series, following Mega Man 11 from 2018. Also announced at The Game Awards 2025, the game reportedly takes a classic approach to the Blue Bomber (run, shoot, slide, and battle colorful, dangerous robots) while featuring a refreshed visual style. Alongside the announcement, CAPCOM launched a Robot Master design contest, giving fans the chance to submit ideas for a boss character to appear in the game; this is a tradition that goes back to earlier entries in the series. The game is set for 2027, the same year the Mega Man series celebrates its 40th anniversary.

Which of these games are you most excited to see at the imminent CAPCOM Spotlight?

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/capcom-spotlight-returns-march-5-pragmata-monster-hunter-stories-3/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Amazon’s God of War Series Adds Four Additional Characters
item.content:encoded:
The cast of Amazon’s upcoming God of War live-action series continues to grow, with four more characters added to the series.

Prime Video‘s series, which will follow the Norse themes of the 2018 God of War title, has begun development, and while no official release date has been set, news continues to filter out from production.

Four More Characters Added to God of War Series

As reported by Eurogamer, the God of War series has expanded Thor’s lineage with the casting of four characters, bringing Modi, Magni, Gna, and Thrud into the series.

Thor’s brothers, Modi and Magni, will be played by Louis Cunningham and Ben Chapple, while Evelyn Miller joins the show as Gna, Odin’s Valkyrie commander, and Island Austin will fill the role of Thor’s daughter Thrud.

According to Variety, Cunningham and Austin will be series regulars, while Chapple and Miller, in the roles of Magni and Gna, will have recurring roles.

Cunningham was a series regular in Marie Antoinette and had one appearance in Bridgerton, while Chapple is known for his role as Liam Flynn on Playing for Keeps.

Meanwhile, Miller’s previous appearances come from the likes of Gangs of London and Doctor Who, while Austin has a slew of upcoming releases, including Insidious: The Bleeding World.

The quartet join a packed cast in the God of Wars series, which features Ryan Hurst as Kratos, Ed Skrein as Baldur, Teresa Palmer as Sif, Callum Vinson as Atreus, Mandy Patinkin as Odin, and Olafur Darri Olaffsson as Thor.

What are your impressions of the growing cast in the God of War series? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more God of War, take a look at claims of a new spin-off title, and check out the first look at Kratos and Atreus in costume.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/amazons-god-of-war-series-adds-four-additional-characters/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Capcom Spotlight announced for this week, with Pragmata and more to feature
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has announced a new showcase, scheduled for later this week.

Capcom Spotlight, as it is known, will air on Thursday, 5th March, with things kicking off at 2pm PT. That's 9pm for those of us here in the UK.

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

The stream will last for roughly half an hour, the studio has said, and give us all the "latest information about Capcom's upcoming releases".

And what upcoming releases are those, Victoria? Well, Capcom's March Spotlight will include a look at the following games:

  • Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection
  • Pragmata
  • Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection
  • Mega Man Dual Override
  • Street Fighter 6

You can check out a short teaser for the upcoming Capcom Spotlight via the video below.

After the game being somewhat MIA for many years, Capcom is really keen to make sure we don't forget about Pragmata. The astronaut and small girl-starring game was once planned for a 2022 release, having been originally revealed in 2020. Now, after a few delays, it is slated to release this April. There is currently a demo for Pragmata available on Steam.

"The result is a fascinating mix that was only better in its more difficult and more thought-provoking proper hands-on than it was in the two previous proof-of-concept style demos," Alex Donaldson wrote in Eurogamer's Pragmata preview.

Pragmata is set to arrive on 24th April, across PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC and Switch 2.

Pragmata

PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC, Nintendo Switch 2

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/capcom-spotlight-announced-for-this-week-with-pragmata-and-more-to-feature
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Well, that looks like it could be a Fallout: New Vegas remaster tease from a Fallout 76 support studio
item.content:encoded:
Cue the line enquiring whether that's a punt in the cranium

Have you ever noticed your next company-wide meeting's due and thought the following: 'Oh, we should post an image prominently featuring a screen from a game rumoured to potentially be getting the remake or remaster treatment'? Well, that appears to have been the case for Iron Galaxy Studios, co-developers of Skyrim's Switch port and support studio on Fallout 76. It's a screen from Fallout: New Vegas to boot.

As spotted by GamesRadar, Iron Galaxy's official account on various social media platforms posted an image with the 'Please Stand By' bit of New Vegas' bootup sequence front and centre. "Today’s our February company meeting. It’s time to catch up with what the company’s been up to and what’s coming up next for IG," reads the caption of these posts, which went up on the evening of February 26th.

That doesn't seem designed to tease something teasily at all. Heck, as you can see in the second monitor in the picture, it's just slide number one of 72 in a meeting presentation. Clearly just a means for some exec to tell folks to settle down and crack a joke before getting into all the very engaging numbers chat. As for the Chicago-based studio posting this to all of their socials, there's no way they could have anticipated folks might interpret it as some sort of remaster or remake tease, right? Fallout, of course, is a series renowned for rarely being the topic of speculation.

Today’s our February company meeting. It’s time to catch up with what the company’s been up to and what’s coming up next for IG.

[image or embed]

— Iron Galaxy Studios (@irongalaxystudios.com) 26 February 2026 at 20:56

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

It's worth noting that - while there's clearly nothing going on here - Iron Galaxy would be the sort of studio that'd seem to make sense to be working on Fallout remasters. In addition to their aforementioned credits on Skyrim and Fallout 76, they've worked on the VR iterations of the fifth Scrolling Elder and Fallout 4. That's in addition to developing the likes of last year's Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3 + 4 and co-developing The Last of Us Part 2 Remastered.

While New Vegas remaster chatter's picked up around the release of the Fallout TV Show's second series, there's arguably been more plentiful smoke around the idea of Fallout 3 getting the remaster treatment to this point, dating all the way back to a Microsoft document leak in 2023. It would be quite funny if Iron Galaxy turned out to have included the wrong 'Please Stand By' screen in this definitely not a teaser.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/well-that-looks-like-it-could-be-a-fallout-new-vegas-remaster-tease-from-a-fallout-76-support-studio
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Tuesday, March 3
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Tuesday, March 3, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (March 3)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for March 3, 2026.

Across

1 – When tripled, “That’s correct!”

5 – “F1” or “One Battle After Another”

6 – Make up for one’s sins

7 – Title filmn character who says “That’ll do, Donkey. That’ll do”

8 – Pocket Janglers

Down

1 – Words before “math” or “honors”

2 – What the world’s first chess set was carved from

3 – Highest digits in Sudoku

4 – Nerd

5 – Cover up

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (March 3)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on March 3, 2026.

Across

1 – Sound a doorbell makes, ___ dong

5 – Something you watch at the cinema

6 – Make amends

7 – Voiced by Michael Myers

8 – Crucial part of a piano

Down

1 – 1989 movie featuring Spike Lee

2 – Elephant tusk

3 – Near-perfect ratings

4 – Expert in something ‘uncool’

5 – Something a Mexican wrestler wears

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (March 3)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on March 3, 2026.

Across

1 – DING

5 – MOVIE

6 – ATONE

7 – SHREK

8 – KEYS

Down

1 – DOTHE

2 – IVORY

3 – NINES

4 – GEEK

5 – MASK

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more word game help, take a look at hints for today’s Contexto, and check out tips for today’s Connections.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-mini-crossword-march-3-hints/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Amazon's God of War casting adds several of Thor's children, as well as Odin's "fanatical" Valkyrie commander
item.content:encoded:
Things that go Thrud in the night.

Four more names have been added to the cast of Amazon's upcoming God of War live-action series, with Thor's lineage being well represented.

Thor's sons Modi and Magni will be played by Louis Cunningham and Ben Chapple respectively. Meanwhile, Evelyn Miller will star as Gna - Odin's Valkyrie commander - and Island Austin as Thor's daughter Thrud. According to Variety, Cunningham and Austin will be series regulars, while Chapple and Miller will have recurring roles in God of War.

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

The Asgard foursome join other confirmed cast members including Ryan Hurst as Kratos, Callum Vinson as Atreus, Teresa Palmer as Phoebe/Sif, Max Parker as Heimdall, Mandy Patinkin as Odin, Ed Skrein as Baldur and Ólafur Darri Ólafsson the Norse God of Thunder, Thor.

As for their official character descriptions, you can check them out below:

Louis Cunningham as Modi: Sif and Thor's middle child, Modi has a gentle disposition and an obsessive, almost insatiably curious mind. He loves puzzles and complex problems - to such an extent that he often gets lost within them and forgets everything else around him.

Ben Chapple as Magni: Sif and Thor's eldest son. Handsome and adventurous, Magni is an excellent hunter and natural ladies’ man, but the life of an immortal playboy has begun to feel hollow and Magni is yearning for a greater purpose. He is extremely protective of his younger brother, Modi.

Evelyn Miller as Gna: Gna is Odin's Valkyrie commander. A fierce warrior, she is devoted to her duty with a loyalty to Odin that borders on fanatical. Highly disciplined and uncompromising, she is dedicated to serving the realm, occasionally at the expense of her own wellbeing.

Island Austin as Thrud: Sif and Thor's youngest child, Thrud is precocious, inquisitive, curious, and fearless, with a natural yearning for knowledge. She's gentle and kind, and often acts as a peace-maker with the rest of her troubled family. Her grandfather, Odin, sees seeds of greatness in Thrud (mostly because she reminds him of himself) and the two have a special bond.

"God of War follows father and son Kratos and Atreus as they embark on a journey to spread the ashes of their wife and mother, Faye. Through their adventures, Kratos tries to teach his son to be a better god, while Atreus tries to teach his father how to be a better human," reads the show's official logline. We still don't have a release window for the first season, however a second season of God of War has already been greenlit at Amazon.

Earlier this month, we got our first look at Hurst and Vinson as Kratos and Atreus, in an image reminiscent of the opening of 2018's God of War.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/amazons-god-of-war-casting-adds-several-of-thors-children-as-well-as-odins-fanatical-valkyrie-commander
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Warframe arrives for Nintendo Switch 2 later this month, alongside a host of new content
item.content:encoded:
One of the most anticipated free-to-play upgrades for the Nintendo Switch 2 has to be Warframe. Digital Extremes’ long-running sci-fi shooter has continually proved that its excellent blend of melee and gunplay, unique character design, and emphasis on gameplay over microtransactions is the way to do free-to-play. The original version was also a real tech showcase for the first Switch, so we’re extremely excited to get our hands on the upgraded version and see just how good it looks on the Switch 2 when it releases on the 25th of March, 2026.

The Switch 2 version is set to launch alongside Warframe’s brand new update, entitled The Shadowgrapher, which brings a bunch of new quests, a new game mode, an expanded storyline, and the game’s 64th Frame in the shape of Follie.

For those hopping into Warframe on the Switch 2, there’s going to be an immediate upgrade, with the game running at 60fps with a 1080p resolution. Alongside that, there will be improved load times, and enhanced textures and shader quality. Given that the game looked surprisingly good on the original Switch, you can fully expect it to make a big jump forward, likely towards the Xbox Series S version, which has become the closest technical benchmark for Nintendo’s console.

The team at Digital Extremes have also added mouse controls thanks to the Joy Con 2’s best, and often forgotten, party trick, and you can use them across Warframe’s menu systems and in gameplay, helping you to line up the perfect headshot.

If you log in to Warframe on a Nintendo Switch 2 console from March 25 to April 15 you can also earn the exclusive Ambimanus Pack, which includes the Vericres Warfan weapon, the Akomeogi Warfan weapon skin, Slicing Feathers Stance Mod, among other cosmetics, and Affinity/Credit boosters. I will be there on day one!

The Shadowgrapher sees Warframe going all Dead by Daylight too, with the new game mode, Follie’s Hunt, bringing a 1v4 asymmetric multiplayer experience to the game. Here, the team of four have to avoid Follie, while completing the unfinished paintings scattered around the ruins of the Vesper Relay. You do this by seeking out pools of coloured ink, coating players in the liquid abyss, and transferring it to scattered canvases. So, a terrifying game of paint-by-numbers?

Each run is set to offer a varied experience due to modifiers that randomise mission hazards and enemy effects. The team say that you’ll have to “remain vigilant and level-headed as the enemy Follie gains shorter ability cooldowns, becoming more aggressive with the completion of each canvas.” Sounds great!

The devstream also gave details on the game’s yearly convention, TennoCon, with 2026 ticket sales beginning on Friday, February 27 at 4 p.m. ET. on Eventbrite:

TennoCon, the annual record-breaking two-day celebration of all things Warframe and Soulframe, will return to Digital Extremes’ hometown of London, Ontario, on July 10-11, 2026. The TennoConcert will also make a highly-requested return, featuring an orchestra performance spanning Warframe’s 13-year discography.

    • Day One (Friday, July 10) will offer a cosplay contest, early merch sales, and autograph signings, concluding with the expanded TennoConcert in Southwestern Ontario’s biggest arena — Canada Life Place.
    • Day Two (Saturday, July 11) will offer dedicated panels around art, music, and narrative, a Soulframe keynote featuring the latest news, more autograph opportunities, as well as the main event, TennoLive. One of Twitch’s most-watched and most-anticipated livestreams each year, TennoLive is the Warframe keynote presentation, promising the biggest announcements and gameplay reveals of the year. Details on full panel schedules to come soon.

All tickets to TennoCon also come with access to the TennoCon 2026 Digital Pack, which includes in-game cosmetics, including emotes, skins, 475 Platinum currency, and more. For more ticket information and pricing details, please visit the official TennoCon Eventbrite ticket page.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/03/warframe-arrives-for-nintendo-switch-2-later-this-month-alongside-a-host-of-new-content/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Today’s Contexto #1262: Hints & Answer For March 3, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, March 3, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: An admission pass
  • Hint 2: Typically purchased for entry
  • Hint 3: Golden _

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Reservation
  • Flight
  • Tour
  • Airline
  • Tix
  • Discount
  • Fare
  • Booking
  • Concert

Today’s Contexto Answer For Game #1262 On March 3, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Ticket

A Ticket is typically a piece of paper or card (or an electronic substitute) that gives the holder a certain right, be it to enter a place, travel somewhere, or participate in an event.

When Is The Next Contexto Game After #1262?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on March 4

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Check out some of Insider Gaming’s other word-game guides down below:

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/todays-contexto-1262-hints-answer-for-march-3-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sumerian Six Console Review – Top tier tactics
item.content:encoded:
For fans of isometric action stealth real-time puzzle games – the acronym for the genre is frankly an absolute mess – the news of Mimimi Games’ closure was like a gut punch to the face – if that combination of conflicting body parts even makes sense. No more Shadow Tactics, no Desperados 4, no sequel to Shadow Gambit; Mimimi’s untimely departure was a crushing loss to the video game industry. Which is why Sumerian Six grabbed me by the eyeballs during its initial release in 2024.

Looking like the forgotten lovechild of Mimimi, this release from Artificer Games gave me serious Shadow Gambit vibes, just with soldiers rather than pirates. But now I’ve finally got my grubby mitts on the PS5 release of the game – I really must stop playing games wearing gloves and wash them instead – can Sumerian Six possibly live up to the unrelenting hype of my own mind? Well, yes.

Set in an alternative history of World War 2, Sumerian Six tells the story of the Enigma Squad, a sextet of scientists seeking to save the world from occult loving Nazis. They’ll do this by visiting different German-occupied bases – castles, underground bunkers, demon-summoning laboratories, you get the idea – sneak around and kill almost everyone they meet. Objectives amount to navigating the labyrinthine lairs you face to reach a certain location. Though within that structure, there’s an impressive amount of player strategies available. Indeed, the amount of tactical options available to the player, thanks to six player characters and numerous innovative abilities, is rather mind-boggling.

Levels are vast, but are smartly broken down into a series of smaller challenge rooms, each providing the player with a puzzle: How do I get my team safely past the sentries guarding the way? With the smart, accessible, and well-thought-through controller setup, you’re nimbly able to highlight the view cones, sight lines, and movement patterns of your enemy, figuring out the optimum route through their ranks. Of course, you’ll get spotted and killed a lot, but quick saves can be liberally applied with the tap of a button, minimising the frustration. Though, unlike some stealth games, if you are spotted, there’s really no getting out of it, you’re best-off restarting as dozens of Nazis close in on your position and shoot the Sumerian out of your six.

Speaking of the Sumerian Six, it’s thanks to the inventive abilities of your team that the gameplay remains as interesting and engaging as it does through the twenty-hour ish playtime. One of your scientists can swap positions with a nearby enemy, setting them up for an ambush, whilst another can travel around sat on an enemy, unseen – kind of like a budget-priced Antman. There are time-limited invisibility suits to experiment with, various stun grenades, and a dude who turns into a Nazi-eviscerating werebear.

Part of the fun is in discovering how these distinctive abilities play off with each other, combining their effects in unusual and memorable ways. Thanks to the ability to pause time and give your squad individual orders, you can easily set up elaborate traps for your slightly-too dumb foes. Body-swapping with a clueless henchman, who is then sent into the welcoming arms of an enormous man bear, is an early highlight, but the creative delights keep on coming.

Sure, the enemy can be stupid, but I genuinely didn’t mind that they quickly resume their patrol despite all their friends being dead, it just gets the player back into the sneaking sooner. After all, Sumerian Six is a pacey stealth game – you can even speed up time to get to your desired moment to strike faster – and the fact that the enemy don’t spend much time searching for you adds to its rollicking rapid rhythm. They make up for their inherent idiocy with numbers, and, for some levels, things can get too grindy with as several nasty difficulty spikes as the game demands you intricately creep through entire armies of enemies.

There’s no two ways about it, to make progress, you will have to keeping trying and failing until you find the right approach. So, in this regard, Sumerian Six certainly won’t be for everyone. Still, with its delightfully pulpy plot, over-the-top comic book visuals, and eminently innovative character abilities for die-hard Mimimi fans, there’s plenty here to keep you delighting in culling the Nazi-herd for many hours to come.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/03/sumerian-six-console-review-top-tier-tactics/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New GTA 6 ‘Leak’ Contains Footage Forgotten For Years
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6’s prospective fanbase has busied itself poring over a short snippet of the game uncovered in a recent claimed leak. It’s recent in that it was uploaded just one day ago, but according to the user who posted the clip, it’s more than four years old and was captured by a former Rockstar employee during the COVID-19-induced lockdown.

This clip predates the 2022 leaks that uncovered around 100 GB of footage and data from GTA 6, and while it’s short and sweet, it reveals a couple of minor details.

GTA 6 ‘Bridge Leak’ Emerges on Instagram

On Instagram, user ‘vice.city’alligator’ posted a few seconds of footage from an early build of Grand Theft Auto 6. In the clip’s description, the user explained that the quality is ‘absolute s***e’ because it was sent via email years ago and was subjected to intense compression.

They explained: ‘Never thought I’d actually post it cuz its a nothingburger.’

The user then stepped away from the account and requested that nobody ask them any questions because they simply wouldn’t be around to answer them.

Per the claim, the clip was sent to the uploader years ago by a former Rockstar employee. It was ‘recorded straight from a dev’s setup during lockdown’, and it shows a bridge over a large inlet, over which a Pißwasser beer truck drives. In the distance, we can see another bridge that seems to have some vehicular traffic coasting over it, which gives us a glimpse at some of the game’s intended scale.

We also see some other content, including a plane coursing through the sky at a substantial height, and a speedboat zipping by just beyond the bridge.

It’s not much, but that’s because it’s years old.

From what we know, Grand Theft Auto 6 is still on track to be released on November 19. There’s every chance it could be delayed again, but Take-Two has now reiterated on numerous occasions that the date is locked in.

Are you excited to play GTA 6 this year? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marathon developers are considering duo lobbies

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/new-gta-6-leak-contains-footage-forgotten/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon Developers Considering Dedicated Duo Lobbies
item.content:encoded:
In the final feedback form published by Bungie after the Marathon server slam, the team revealed an investigation into the potential for duo-exclusive lobbies. In Marathon, users can play solo, queue as a duo, or play as a trio, but there’s no dedicated duo option.

This has been picked up by Bungie as a point of contention, with many users requesting the feature to give themselves an easier time of things. Being a duo against guaranteed trios is never fun, and prospective fans of the new extraction title want to level the playing field a little.

Marathon Duos Could Be Coming at Launch

Marathon is set to hit the market in just two days, building on the semi-success of the recent server slam test. The ‘demo’ pulled in tens of thousands of players and racked up high viewership figures, but some pointed out that the player count withered pretty quickly.

In a post on social media, Bungie summed up everything it had learned from the server slam, uncovering everything the team is working on before the game launches. That includes the complex UI, concerns around PvP cadence, the economy system, and issues with ‘heat generation’ and general movement mechanics.

Another thing that was talked about was ‘Additional queue options‘.

The team wrote:

Currently you have the ability to run as a Solo (be it as Rook or as a Runner in a solo lobby), or you can queue in with a duo or a full trio – but we have heard the feedback about duo dedicated lobbies. This has been logged and shared with the team.

This isn’t any kind of promise that duo lobbies will surface, but it’s one step closer for those who want the feature. Admittedly, strict lobby sizing of this nature is something more reserved for battle royale titles, as most extraction games promote an unpredictable model that ensures you never know the size of the team you’re running into.

The best example of this is Escape from Tarkov, the spearhead extraction title that has been around for almost a decade. In Escape from Tarkov, you can run into a solo player or a five-man stack in the same lobby without any prior knowledge. The game doesn’t pick and choose who is matched with whom based on team sizes.

Would you want to see a duo feature implemented in Marathon? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that legacy Minecraft source code has leaked

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: The Best RPG in Years Has Officially Joined Xbox Game Pass
item.content:encoded:
Little more than a year after release, Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 has joined the Xbox Game Pass service, presenting subscribers with one hell of a medieval marvel. Last year, Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 emerged to a stunning reception, receiving critical acclaim worldwide and going on to win some of the industry’s most prestigious awards.

In our review, we awarded the game perfect marks, and for good reason. As one of the most expansive, detailed, and immersive open-world RPG titles to be released in years, KCD2 deserved every shred of recognition it got.

And now, you can play it for free as part of your Game Pass subscription.

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 is On Game Pass

We learned about KCD2’s imminent Game Pass arrival midway through February, but it’s only now that the game has officially landed on the platform. Users on Xbox and PC can download KCD2 for no extra cost, soaking up all that Warhorse’s RPG epic has to offer – provided you’re not on the Essential tier, that is.

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 was one of 2025’s most decorated games. The title spawned an immense following, building on the success of the first game, which dropped in 2018. The follow-up title continued the story laid out in the first game, but blew it wide open, introducing an enormous world, hours upon hours of content, and some incredible acting, amongst other things.

It went on to receive three major expansions inside a year, showing immense cadence from Warhorse Studios.

Kingdom Come has joined the Game Pass service alongside Final Fantasy 3, a classic RPG that surfaced way back in 1990.

Do you intend to download Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 and give it a try? Let us know what you’re planning on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that old Minecraft source code has leaked

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Minecraft Source Code Leak Reveals Long-Forgotten Features
item.content:encoded:
Minecraft has suffered a massive leak that has uncovered legacy source code from way back in 2014. This code is tied directly to the game’s older versions, specifically those made for the PS3, PS4, Xbox 360, and Xbox One, and dataminers have busied themselves uncovering every shred of information possible from what’s available.

There’s even information pertaining to the PS Vita port, which was one of the most short-lived versions of the game, given the console’s relatively contracted lifespan.

Minecraft Legacy Leak Uncovers Expired Source Code

Tech-minded social media users have been sharing all kinds of content from the Minecraft source code leak that hit the airwaves just a day or two ago. They’ve been trying to rebuild these old versions of Minecraft, the likes of which have long since disappeared from storefronts worldwide.

The older builds that they’ve managed to dig into contain forgotten or failed elements that Mojang and 4J Studios abandoned, such as floating islands and visual effects for waterfalls.

On Reddit, users discussed the implications of the source code leak, with one user writing:

Does this mean there is a chance we can now use modern blocks, mobs, & items in 360 edition?

Thus, we don’t need to buy Bedrock or Java to use modern items?

One of the more tech-savvy users reported that ‘work is already underway and they’ve even managed to run the code on an RGH-modded Xbox 360.’ This was after a Discord server was circulated, populated by folks looking to make the most of the leak. Later reports suggest the Discord server was banned.

Another user explained, when asked if this will ‘make Minecraft free’:

I highly doubt it, all this will do is finally let others make mods for the ps3/xbox360 versions of the game, or make custom title updates with things say from newer versions of the game. This leak will more than likely not affect the current version of Minecraft.

In one shot uploaded to Reddit, user PhoenixSC revealed a file repository that showed the extent of what had been uncovered. The expansive breakdown included several gigabytes of data.

Are you eager to get your hands on the legacy source code for Minecraft? Let us know if you’re interested in the retro versions of the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PUBG Black Budget has been cracked by dataminers

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Massacre Codes (March 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox Massacre monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of rewards, including lots of free credits.

Massacre is a Roblox horror survival experience that assigns players to either Survivors or a Killer. The Killer must hunt down and eliminate every survivor without mercy, while the Survivors must run, hide, and fight to stay alive by any means necessary. This intense asymmetric horror game transforms each match into a deadly game of hide-and-seek where every heartbeat could be your last.

Players can also purchase gamepasses to enhance their experience, including items like Permanent Bandages, Bear Traps, Bloxy Cola, and Pepper Spray, as well as abilities like Push and Super Push to gain tactical advantages. Created by Abyssal Covenant and released about two years ago, Massacre has attracted over 5 million visits.

Although items and advantages are earned through gameplay, the fastest way to gain an edge is by redeeming promo codes that developers release. These codes grant free rewards and boosts so players can improve their survival chances and unlock exclusive items instead of grinding through countless terrifying matches.

Active Massacre Codes

Expired Massacre Codes

  • omg500k
  • smallbugfix
  • tyeveryone
  • 400k
  • 500members
  • 300ktuff
  • byebyebye
  • perks
  • 200kwow
  • loveyall
  • Meow

How to redeem Massacre Codes

Use the following steps to activate all the codes in Massacre Codes:

  1. Boot Massacre in Roblox and spawn into the game
  2. Click on the cog/gear icon on the left-hand side of the screen
  3. Scroll to the bottom and find the code redemption text box
  4. Type or paste in the code
  5. Then click on enter to claim the freebie

Where to get more Massacre Codes

Massacre codes are typically released through the game’s official community and developer channels, but they do not appear automatically in-game. The best places to catch them early are the official Massacre Discord server, which usually has an announcements or codes channel where new codes are shared first, and Abyssal Covenant’s social media pages, where updates, milestones, and event rewards for the game are announced.

Codes often come out around major updates, player milestones, or special events. Still, if you only check occasionally, it’s very easy to miss them since Massacre does not notify players when a new code drops or when one expires. Because of this, the easiest strategy is to save this guide in your browser and check back instead of trying to monitor every source yourself.

This page is refreshed as soon as new codes go live, and any expired codes are removed promptly, so you always see only the ones that are still active.

Enjoying our Roblox games codes coverage? Check out the codes for popular games like Steal A Brainrot, Radiant Residents, and Fix It Up on Insider Gaming!

Interested in the latest developments in the world of gaming and esports? Then join the Insider Gaming Discord server and engage with the passionate community.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/roblox-massacre-codes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pokémon Center Confirms Anniversary Pikachu Plush Restocks
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon is celebrating its 30th anniversary in all sorts of ways, and one of the most in-demand products is the "Beginning Pikachu" plush, available at the Pokémon Center.

At the moment, this retro Pikachu inspired the Pokémon's pixel design and look from the Game Boy generation, is exclusive to Japan, but it's already sold out. Scalpers haven't helped the situation, but thankfully, this anniversary item is returning.

As highlighted by VGC, The Pokémon Company has confirmed it will be restocking this item as a pre-order purchase, so in other words, Pokémon fans won't be forced to pay extreme resale prices and can instead wait for an official Pokémon Center restock to come through.

The key chain, which is based on the same retro Pikachu design, will also be getting restocked in-store and on the website.

Select 30th anniversary merchandise has been made available on the Pokémon Center locally, but at the moment, there's no sign of this retro-inspired Pikachu plush. So, if it is released here, based on this update, it seems there could be a chance of restocks.

Would you be interested in this Pikachu plush? Let us know in the comments.



https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/03/pokemon-center-confirms-anniversary-pikachu-plush-restocks
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PUBG Black Budget Cracked by Dataminer Who Reveals Test Maps
item.content:encoded:
PUBG: Black Budget is one of this year’s more highly anticipated extraction shooters. It underwent a closed alpha in December that drew in a few thousand players, all eager to see how the game would stack up alongside the likes of ARC Raiders and Escape from Tarkov.

Black Budget has promise, but it’s built around a confusing blend of battle royale and extraction gameplay mechanics that might put some players off. Regardless, dataminers are hard at work finding out what they can about the game, and one user on social media recently reported cracking the KRAFTON-backed title wide open.

Black Budget Broken Into

On social media, user Shawn Weber reported cracking Black Budget, forcing the game to load and to play matches locally, despite the title being offline since the playtest closed midway through December.

In a follow-up post, the dataminer uncovered images of developer test maps, showcasing a series of environments in which the game’s creators could demo character models, weapons, movement mechanics, and more.

One of the shots included some gnarled creature, the kind that wasn’t seen in the game’s demo, which proved to be a fairly barren experience, devoid of most forms of life.

Black Budget has a unique premise, but it might end up getting lost in the complexity of the thing. In this extraction game, an anomaly is swallowing the game map over time, slowly strangling extraction points and key areas of the game world.

Most of the exfiltration in the game is done via dynamic wormholes that can be generated at the press of a button, eradicating ‘fixed’ extraction sites that we’ve seen for years in competing extraction games.

It fuses PUBG’s battle royale mechanics with KRAFTON’s new extraction shooter design.

Black Budget doesn’t have a release date, nor is there any indication of when the next demo will arrive, but Weber might busy himself digging deep into the game’s files to see what else he can uncover.

Did you enjoy Black Budget’s demo? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marathon’s UI might not change too much

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Pokémon Scarlet & Violet Announces Final Ranked Battle Season
item.content:encoded:
Another generation winds down

Following last week's reveal of the next major Pokémon game Pokémon Winds & Waves (and a release window for the battle title Pokémon Champions), The Pokémon Company has officially announced it will be winding down Pokémon Scarlet & Violet's seasonal Ranked Battle content.

The final season for the Switch game "Season 41" will officially kick off on 1st April 2026. After this goes live, Ranked Battles will no longer receive updates. This season will run until the game's servers are taken offline, and there will be no monthly rankings or rewards for rankings.

This announcement follows the recent conclusion of Season 39 and the start of Season 40. Trainers can now collect rewards for Season 39. As for Season 40, it runs from now until 31st March 2026. You can see the full ruleset for this season on Serebii.net.

If you haven't already tried out Pokémon Scarlet & Violet on the Switch 2, these titles received a free upgrade last June.

Did you participate in Scarlet & Violet's Ranked Battles? How do you feel about Season 41 being the final season. Let us know in the comments.

[source serebii.net]



https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/03/pokemon-scarlet-and-violet-announces-final-ranked-battle-season
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon Developer Says The UI Won’t Have Its ‘Sauce’ Removed
item.content:encoded:
Marathon launches in just a couple of days, following the semi-successful server slam that took place last weekend. The testing period drew in hundreds of thousands of players across all platforms, stress-testing the game for Bungie and uncovering some of the game’s most critical issues.

One of those more glaring issues concerned the game’s UI. It was found to be overly complex by many, boasting unnecessary visuals, far too much clutter, and awkward navigational elements. Bungie has said it’ll iterate on the UI, but one of the game’s UI designers has suggested that won’t happen.

What’s The Deal With ‘Fontslop’?

Marathon’s user interface designer, Elliott Gray, recently popped up on social media in response to a UI ‘redesign’ by a Marathon player. The redesign in question oversimplified the game’s layout, which drew a comment from the self-proclaimed ‘fontslop merchant’.

If there’s one leading debate amongst Marathon players, it’s that the game boasts a needlessly complex UI and visual palette, and that naturally includes the many fonts that make up the game’s front end. It’s a little bit too cacophonous for some.

When user LukeCB14 posted an image of the ‘simplified’ Marathon, Gray wrote:

all jokes aside there’s plenty of work we can and will do as a team to respond to player feedback about inventory management, navigation, density of info, etc…

…but don’t think for a second that we’re gonna remove the SAUCE from the UI.

Marathon’s former art director, Joseph Cross, wrote on social media that the game boasted ‘an embarrassment of riches’ in the graphics space. He parted ways with the project last year, but has remained positive about the game’s delivery since.

Gray’s post has racked up almost 200,000 impressions and generated a widespread debate regarding Marathon’s UI… Again. It has been recognized as one of the biggest points of contention by Bungie, leading the studio to promise a reiteration of the appearance post-launch, but to what depth, nobody knows.

Many have argued that Marathon’s full visual palette is one of the only things that’ll help it stand out from the competition in the extraction space.

Do you like how Marathon looks? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a new Fallout developer might have revealed itself

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Capcom Spotlight Set for March 5
item.content:encoded:
A Capcom Spotlight presentation is set for this Thursday, March 5. The company announced the upcoming presentation on social media.

Among the games featured will be Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, PRAGMATA, Street Fighter 6, Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection, and Mega Man Dual Override.

Dual Override is one of the only games announced that doesn’t have a release date. It was first shown off at the 2025 Game Awards. Monster Hunter will be released next Friday, March 13. Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection will be released 2 weeks later, on March 27.

PRAGMATA is slated for release on April 24, and Street Fighter 6 has been on the market for quite some time.

The event will be broadcast at 2:00 p.m. PST and 5:00 p.m. on the East Coast. The presentation will be about 35 minutes in length. It’s also noted that it will be broadcast in 13 languages and that the date and time can change without notice. The presentation will also feature certain age-restricted titles.

Capcom is currently firing on all cylinders following the release of Resident Evil Requiem. The game is one of the best-reviewed titles of 2025 and an early contender for game of the year. Check out how to solve that game’s final puzzle here.

What are you hoping to see at the next Capcom Spotlight event? What kind of surprise games, if any, do you think there will be?

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/capcom-spotlight-set-for-march-5/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Capcom Spotlight Taking Place Later This Week, Here's A Teaser Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Expect Mega Man's new outing and multiple other titles

Nintendo has already got an Indie World showcase taking place this week, and now Capcom has announced it will be holding a special 'Spotlight' broadcast later this week on 5th March 2026.

This Spotlight will run for approximately 30 minutes and will include news for Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, Pragmata, Mega Man Star Force: Legacy Collection and Street Fighter 6. According to Capcom's website, one of the other "featured titles" will be Mega Man: Dual Override, launching next year. Here's the official announcement:

Thursday, March 5, 2026 Streaming from 2:00 p.m. (PST)
(Thursday, March 5, 2026 at 10:00 p.m. (UTC))

"The Capcom Spotlight is a digital event that brings you the latest news from Capcom! Tune in for the latest information about Capcom's upcoming releases. This broadcast will have subtitles available in 13 languages."

Capcom Spotlight - March 2026

  • North America: 2pm PST / 3pm MST / 4pm CST / 5pm EST
  • UK/Ire: 10pm GMT
  • Europe: 11pm CET / 12am (Fri) EET
  • Asia/Oceania: 7am (Fri) JST / 6am (Fri) AWST / 9am (Fri) AEDT

Capcom hasn't shared any additional information about what to expect. Monster Hunter Stories 3 and Mega Man Star Force arrive next month alongside the Alex DLC for Street Fighter 6. Pragmata will then follow these titles on in April, and at some point next year the new Mega Man game is making its way to the Switch and Switch 2.

This spotlight follows Capcom's release of Resident Evil Requiem and two Gold Editions on the Switch 2 last week.

As usual, we'll be sure to share all the Switch and Switch 2-related news from this event. What game are you most looking forward to hearing more about in this upcoming broadcast? Tell us in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/03/capcom-spotlight-taking-place-later-this-week-heres-a-teaser-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Kirby Air Riders Races Onto Nintendo Music This Week
item.content:encoded:
Listen to the full soundtrack today

Nintendo previously released a sample of the Kirby Air Riders soundtrack on Nintendo Music last year, and in a new update today, it's now made the entire album available.

This is the first proper Switch 2 soundtrack release on the mobile app and comes loaded with 114 tracks and has a runtime of three hours. This includes tracks such as Starlit Journey, Floria Fields and much more.

The main composers of the album are Noriyuki Iwadare (known for series such as Grandia, Lunar, Super Smash Bros.) and Shogo Sakai (Mother 3, Smash Bros), with support from Akira Miyagawa and multiple Bandai Namco musicians. Timed in with this album drop today, Nintendo has released an interview featuring Iwadare and Sakai, if you want to know more about how the soundtrack was composed for Kirby Air Riders.

To listen to this album on the Nintendo Music app, you'll need to have an active Switch Online membership. Kirby Air Riders also got an update last week bumping it up to Version 1.3.1. It added support for new amiibo and also included balance adjustments and bug fixes.

What do you think of the latest soundtrack to join Nintendo Music? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/03/kirby-air-riders-races-onto-nintendo-music-this-week
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Capcom Spotlight set for March 5, featuring Mega Man: Dual Override, PRAGMATA, and more
item.content:encoded:
30 minutes of updates on upcoming Capcom titles.

Capcom will host a Capcom Spotlight broadcast on March 5 at 2:00 p.m. PT / 5:00 p.m. ET / June 27 at 7:00 JST, the company announced. You will be able to watch it on YouTube (English, Japanese).

The 30-minute broadcast will feature “the latest information about Capcom’s upcoming releases.” Featured titles include Mega Man: Dual Override (the reveal of the Excellence Awards winners for the Robot Master Design Contest), Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection (information on online features), Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, PRAGMATA, and Street Fighter 6.

Watch a teaser trailer below.

Teaser Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/capcom-spotlight-set-for-march-5-featuring-mega-man-dual-override-pragmata-and-more
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Story of Seasons: Grand Bazaar coming to PS5, Xbox Series on May 28
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Xbox Series

PlayStation 5 physical edition also available.

Marvelous will release Story of Seasons: Grand Bazaar for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series on May 28, the company announced.

Story of Seasons: Grand Bazaar first launched for Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam on August 27.

The PlayStation 5 version will be available both physically and digitally worldwide, with the physical edition exclusive to Amazon in the United States.

The standard physical and digital editions will cost $49.99 / £44.99 / €49.99, while the following digital editions will also be available:

  • Digital Deluxe Edition ($59.99 / £49.99 / €59.99) – Includes the base game and the “Trunk of Transformation” cosmetic downloadable content
  • Super Digital Deluxe Edition ($69.99 / £54.99 / €69.99) – Includes the “Trunk of Transformation” cosmetic downloadable content, a digital soundtrack, and digital art book
  • Digital Edition Bonus – All digital editions will also come with the “Cow Set” cosmetic downloadable content as a purchase bonus

Previously available downloadable content will also be available at launch on these new platforms.

Here is an overview of the game, via Marvelous:

About

Zephyr Town’s bazaar was once one of the grandest in the world, drawing customers and peddlers from all four corners of the globe. These days, though, the bazaar has more tumbleweeds than customers. It’s up to Zephyr Town’s newest resident to turn its fortunes around! Players will raise animals, harvest crops, craft rare delicacies, then sell wares at their very own stall in the bazaar. As the bazaar business grows, new goods and services will arrive, which players can use to improve their farm. Help return prosperity to the bazaar, settle down for a cozy life and start a family, and Zephyr Town is sure to thrive once again!

Inspired by the classic handheld title, the new Story of Seasons: Grand Bazaar builds up and improves on the original in every conceivable way. The world is bigger and more alive than ever thanks to incredible new visuals, new characters, an expanded story, and full character voice work in story events—a series first. Take advantage of Zephyr Town’s unique weather, using the wind to make travel and farm tasks a breeze! The winds of change are coming to deliver a new life in Story of Seasons: Grand Bazaar.

Key Features

  • Cozy Living Reaches New Heights – The magnificent beauty of Zephyr Town shines with gorgeous graphics while a richly voiced story carries the player on a rewarding journey of farming, friendship, and renewal.
  • Take the Leap – Harness the power of the wind! Use windmills to create new products to sell, and effortlessly travel across the town on a glider. Use new jumping skills on the farm, or when exploring the town. You never know what you’ll find!
  • Ring the Bell and Sell, Sell, Sell – Your farm’s produce and goods take on additional importance as the heart of the business at the lively weekly bazaar. Customize the stall and take charge of sales to build up buzz and power up profits.
  • A Beautifully Cozy Life – Enjoy a rich life amongst a colorful community of characters, from friendly townsfolk to magical Nature Sprites. Build a home, grow relationships, and even find love and start a family.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series Announce Trailer

English

Japanese

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/story-of-seasons-grand-bazaar-coming-to-ps5-xbox-series-on-may-28
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Server Slams, Assassin's Creed Upheaval & Skate Layoffs: All The News From February 23-March 1
item.content:encoded:
Also: a British games show is in big trouble

Published: March 02, 2026, 7:06 pm

Welcome to Recap, our weekly feature--running every Monday morning--where we round up what’s been going on around video games, the internet and beyond.

Marathon had a little open beta/playtest last week, which quite a few of us checked out. Bungie called it a 'Server Slam', and it ran across Steam, PS5 and Xbox Series X/S.

Veteran developer Clint Hocking, who was working as director on the upcoming Assassin's Creed: Hexe project, has left not just the game but Ubisoft as well.

Full Circle, developers of the latest Skate game, have ben hit by layoffs; it's not known how many of the roughly 200-strong team were affected.

The organizers of British games conference Reboot Develop, Digitalna Avantura, have been "hit with a bankruptcy order over roughly €520,000 in unpaid debts".

I thought I'd seen every Roblox headline I could ever dream to see, and then along comes this:

Nacon, the publisher behind games like Hell is Us, RoboCop, and Test Drive Unlimited, has filed for insolvency.



https://aftermath.site/marall-the-news-from-february-23-march-1/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: GTA Vice City Stories Sales Data Revealed After Trademark Dispute
item.content:encoded:
Sales data for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City Stories has been revealed as Rockstar Games’ parent company, Take-Two Interactive, took action to stop a sandwich shop from naming itself Vice City Subs.

On February 13, 2026, Take-Two Interactive filed an opposition to a trademark for Vice City Subs with the USPTO. The latter would have become a sandwich shop. In the opposition letter, Take-Two tout the success of the Grand Theft Auto brand as a whole, noting that GTA 5 grossed over 1 billion dollars in retail sales in just 3 days after its release back in September 2013.

GTA Vice City Stories Sold Over 7 Million Copies Globally

The Vice City games are also widely regarded as immensely popular. The PlayStation 2 release of Grand Theft Auto: Vice City is noted as having sold dozens of millions of copies worldwide, 17.5 million by 2008. However, the news concerns an updated sales figure for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City Stories, a PlayStation Portable spin-off of the aforementioned PS2 game.

The filing notes that Vice City Stories, first released in 2006, has sold over 7 million copies worldwide. Back in 2008, Take-Two had listed the game as selling 4.5 million copies. This means that the game has sold at least 2.5 million copies in the last 18 years.

The Future of GTA Heads Back to Vice City

As mentioned above, Grand Theft Auto 5 was released nearly 13 years ago in September 2013. Thankfully, the next mainline GTA game is on the way. Grand Theft Auto 6 is expected to be released in November 2026. The game has been delayed numerous times, was once planned for 2025, and, before the most recent delay, was scheduled for May 2026.

This next game will take place in Vice City and is expected to be one of the biggest game releases of its time. Learn about the latest locations planned for the upcoming game at this link.

How do you feel about this action from Take-Two to stop a sandwich shop from using the Vice City name? How excited are you to go back to Vice City in Grand Theft Auto 6? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/gta-vice-city-stories-sales-data-revealed-after-trademark-dispute/
